items.title: Inspect the assembled prompt before sending
items.scenario: Your RAG response is wrong; you suspect the prompt template lost context.
items.explanation: Add `breakpoint()` right before `client.chat.completions.create(...)`. Inspect `messages` — see exactly what the LLM was sent. Done in 30 seconds vs 5 minutes of print + rerun.

items.title: Walk the stack on an unexpected exception
items.scenario: A `KeyError: 'metadata'` deep inside a chunker crashes the pipeline.
items.explanation: Run with `python -m pdb pipeline.py`. After the crash, `w` shows the stack. `u` walks up to the caller. `p locals()` reveals the chunk that's missing metadata. Two minutes; no print-bisection.

items.title: Conditional breakpoint inside a hot loop
items.scenario: 1000 documents stream through; only the 472nd triggers the bug.
items.explanation: Inside the loop: `breakpoint()` guarded by `if doc.id == 'doc-472'`. Or `(Pdb) b chunker.py:42, doc.id == 'doc-472'` from a parent breakpoint. Stops only at the relevant iteration.

items.title: Mutate state and continue
items.scenario: A retry is using a wrong base-URL; you want to test the fix in-place.
items.explanation: At the prompt: `(Pdb) client.base_url = 'https://api.openai.com/v1'`. Then `c` to continue. Verify the fix worked, then make a real code change. No rerun.

items.title: Switch breakpoint() to ipdb without code changes
items.scenario: You want the colour + tab-completion of ipdb but the code uses `breakpoint()`.
items.explanation: Run with `PYTHONBREAKPOINT=ipdb.set_trace python script.py`. Every `breakpoint()` call now lands in ipdb. Disable entirely with `PYTHONBREAKPOINT=0`.
